channel.title: Luka Zoric
channel.description: Notes, essays, and projects by Luka Zoric.

item.title: Note about the posts
item.description: Why I publish: writing is how I measure my own growth, and disagreeing with my past self is the evidence.

item.title: Vectors are multi dimensional numbers
item.description: Building geometric intuition for vectors as multi dimensional numbers, walking through what they are, how to add them, and what scaling does.

item.title: Intuitively understanding derivatives
item.description: Working through what a derivative actually tells us — using a small numeric step, then taking the limit — to build the intuition that it measures how sensitive the output is to a tiny change in the input.

item.title: What are neural networks and how do they work
item.description: A neural network is a giant mathematical expression with adjustable parameters. This post walks through inputs, outputs, loss, derivatives, and gradient descent — how the thing actually learns.

item.title: Intent as foundation
item.description: A compact note: if consciousness is fundamental, intent is the foundation of work — build software that turns unique insights into products.

item.title: Everything is just an interpretation
item.description: My interpretation of Book 1, Chapter 1 of Lao Tzu's Tao Te Ching, on naming, desire, and holding the nameless source and the named world together.

item.title: Building products with LLMs
item.description: Why LLMs are at the limit of their current trajectory, why execution is becoming commoditised, and why your unique insight is the only remaining moat.
